observations.recommendation:
1. The Inventory Committee prioritizes and ensures full implementation of the “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.
2. The Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO/Municipal Treasurer maintains and updates the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) and Property Cards (PC) for proper monitoring and reconciliation.
observations.reason: The municipality is currently unable to initiate the inventory count due to a lack of manpower, the recent retirement of several department heads.

observations.recommendation:
4. The management prioritizes procurement-and/or-implementation-ready projects to avoid unnecessary delays.
5. The management complies with the detailed engineering activities and issues suspension on contractors with negative slippage of 15% and above within the critical path of the project due entirely to the latter’s fault or negligence as provided in Section 17.6 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Projects were clustered to increase bid value and attract contractors. However, this led to logistical issues due to the dispersed locations, inefficient mobilization, and weather disruptions unreflected in PAGASA’s Dauis-based forecasts. Limited pre-construction surveys in remote areas also caused variation orders.

observations.recommendation:
8. The management imposes the withholding of salary of employees with cash advances which remained outstanding beyond the prescribed period.
9. The management sends demand letters to those who are no longer with the agency.
observations.reason: Employees have not responded to demand letters despite repeated reminders and public postings. Partial deductions were only permitted during bonus releases. The management sent demand letters to those who are no longer with the agency.

observations.recommendation:
10. The management stops from purchasing reconditioned motor vehicles moving forward.
11. The management sends written explanation on this procurement.
observations.reason: An additional batch was procured to fulfill the commitment to equip all barangays with vehicles for both operational and emergency purposes.

observations.recommendation:

17. The Municipal Accountant submits the liquidation of all implemented projects and remits the remaining balances thereof.
observations.reason: There are still remaining amounts, but they have already diminished.

observations.recommendation:

19. Moreover, the Municipal Engineer furnishes copies of the building permits and certificate of occupancy to the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the prompt and timely assessment of buildings.
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor has retired, and the position has yet to be filled.

Also, maintain a centralized property registry file with scanned and physical copies of all proofs of ownership of all LGU-owned lots.
Verification of ownership status of 22 public school sites with Dept. of Education. If jointly owned or used, prepare a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with DepEd to outline rights and responsibilities.
Attendance to training for property/asset management and establish a periodic review of property records.

observations.recommendation:
1.2 The audit team once again recommended that the agency to apply for a one-time
cleansing, as outlined in detail in COA Circular No. 2020-006.
1.8 We recommended that management prioritize updating all PPE-related
records and conduct an inventory taking, after which reconciliation, as
outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006, should be made.

2.6 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace the period or date of
acquisition of the assets and compute the depreciation based on IPSAS 17.
2.7 To recognize depreciation for PPE prior to CY 2024:
Debit Credit
Prior Year’s Adjustment Xxx
Accumulated Depreciation Xxx
2.8 To recognize current year depreciation during CY 2024:
Debit Credit
Depreciation Expense Xxx
Accumulated Depreciation Xxx

3.10 We recommended that management send demand letters to those with
unliquidated cash advances and, after which, impose the withholding of
salary of employees with cash advances which remained outstanding beyond
the prescribed period and send demand letters to those who are no longer
with the agency.

4.4 We recommended that management submit a request for relief from
accountability and make the necessary precautions to avoid damages to
the accountable forms.

5.6 We recommended that management faithfully comply with the detailed
engineering activities and make sure to visit the site before preparing the
design and estimates to avoid unnecessary delay on project completion.

6.5 We recommended the management to stop from purchasing reconditioned
motor vehicles moving forward. We also require the management to send
written explanation on this procurement.

7.5 We recommended that Management implement Direct Retail Purchases for
the procurement of POL products and require the end user to request a
Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR)-issued credit invoice or similar document
each time the LGU makes a credit purchase for cross-referencing.
3. The management designates the accountability of cash ticket management to an ETRACS user who shall regularly monitor and reconcile on hand cash ticket collections balances with that of ETRACS.

6. The management faithfully complies with the detailed engineering activities as stated in Annex A of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act 9184 to minimize the need for variation orders but, when necessary, bids out any cumulative positive variation order beyond ten percent (10%) of the original contract price based on the provisions in Annex E of the same Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act 9184.

7. The BAC properly conducts the post-qualification to determine the availability of equipment and personnel committed to the project/s and issues suspension on contractors with negative slippage of 15% and above within the critical path of the project due entirely to its fault or negligence as provided in Appendix 17 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.
12. The management revises the ordinance to fairly respond to the needs of constituents in crisis situations.

13. The LDRRMO submits the Monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF not later than the 15th day after the end of each month to enable the auditor to conduct the timely review, verification and monitoring of the sources and utilization of LDRRMF, thus deficiencies if any, will be informed to management for the latter’s immediate and appropriate action.

14. The management discontinues the practice of granting cash advances to accountable officers in amounts exceeding their corresponding maximum cash accountability. Otherwise, the management increases the fidelity bond of the AO to cater to the maximum cash accountability per cash advance drawn.

15. The management demands the return of mobilization fee from the contractor or payment from the insurance company whichever is faster and convenient. The management blacklists the contractor as a consequence of its default.

26. The property officer traces the property cards of the unserviceable properties and determines the characteristics of the item to facilitate in the identification of its acquisition costs. Once the values of the properties are identified/determined, the property officer pursues the required procedures in disposal of assets in accordance with the aforecited COA Circular 89-296. For unestablished acquisition costs, the Appraisal Committee determines the cost/value of the unserviceable properties.
27. The accountable officials in possession of unserviceable property submits to the Disposal Committee the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP).
28. The property officer deletes from the RPCPPE the IIRUP for condemned properties which are already approved by the Head of Office upon the recommendation of the Property Inspector and/or the Appraisal Committee and cancels the corresponding AREs. Copy of approved IIRUP is furnished to the COA. However, the Accounting Office does not drop the approved IIRUP from the accounting records until such time the Auction Sale is complete.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator facilitate the evaluation of the status of implementation of the funds received from National Government Agencies and other LGUs. Submit the liquidation of all implemented projects and remit the remaining balances thereof.

We recommended that management require the municipal assessor to fast track the inspection and assessment of the real properties so that income collection from the real property tax will increase that can benefit the local government unit.  Moreover, we require the Municipal Engineer to furnish copies of the building permits and certificate of occupancy to the Municipal Assessor to facilitate the prompt and timely assessment of buildings.

We recommended that management, with the assistance of the MPDC and other key players in the decision making of the LGU, strategize so that projects identified have to be implemented within the year to address the needs of the community and to get the maximum value of fund allocated for a specific period considering the increasing prices of materials.  Further, conduct actual studies for previously identified projects and determine if those are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, consider for their reversion to release the funds and make available to some meaningful use.

We recommended that management observe the provisions of COA Circular 2012-003 in spending government funds and ensure funds are spent prudently. We also recommend the amount of P95,000.00 be refunded.

It is required that the Municipal Accountant shall not process claims with incomplete and improper supporting documents. We recommend that management submit the duly certified distribution lists for the relief goods distributed to the victims of the Typhoon and ensure that control procedures and complete documentation are established for claiming against government funds.

We recommended the Management:

Require the property officer to trace the property cards of the unserviceable properties and determine the relevant characteristics of the item to facilitate in the identification of its acquisition costs. Once the values of the properties are now identified/determined, pursue the required procedures in disposal of assets in accordance with the aforecited COA Circular 89-296. For unestablished acquisition costs, the Appraisal Committee shall determine the cost/value of the unserviceable properties.

Direct the accountable officials in possession of unserviceable property to submit to the Disposal Committee the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP).

Delete from the RPCPPE the IIRUP for condemned properties which are already approved by the Head of Office upon the recommendation of the Property Inspector and/or the Appraisal Committee and cancel the corresponding AREs. Copy of approved IIRUP shall be furnished to the COA. However, the Accounting Office shall not drop the approved IIRUP from the accounting records until such time the Auction Sale is complete.

We recommended that the local chief executive require the persons liable to immediately settle the disallowances, otherwise initiate administrative and/or criminal action against them in line with Section 7 of the 2009 RRSA.

We recommended that Management instruct the municipal accountant to review and check the status of the listing/schedule of accounts payable to determine if there are valid claims against these obligated amounts, and if none, these should be reverted immediately to the general fund for subsequent budgeting.

We recommended and the management agreed to require the Inventory Committee to prioritize and ensure full implementation of the “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006. The management also agreed to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO/Municipal Treasurer to maintain and update the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) and Property Cards (PC) for proper monitoring and reconciliation.

We recommended and the management agreed to designate the accountability of cash ticket management to an ETRACS user who shall regularly monitor and reconcile on hand cash ticket balances with that of ETRACS.

We recommended and the management agreed to prioritize procurement-and/or-implementation-ready projects to avoid unnecessary delays. The management also agreed to faithfully comply with the detailed engineering activities and issue suspension on contractors with negative slippage of 15% and above within the critical path of the project due entirely to the latter’s fault or negligence as provided in Section 17.6 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended and the management agreed to faithfully comply with the detailed engineering activities as stated in Annex A of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the Republic Act 9184 to minimize the need for variation orders but, when necessary, bid out any cumulative positive variation order beyond ten percent (10%) of the original contract price based on the provisions in Annex E of the same IRR.

We recommended and the BAC agreed to properly conduct the post-qualification to determine the availability of equipment and personnel committed to the project/s and issue suspension on contractors with negative slippage of 15% and above within the critical path of the project due entirely to its fault or negligence as provided in Appendix 17 of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that management impose the withholding of salary of employees with cash advances which remained outstanding beyond the prescribed period and send demand letters to those who are no longer with the agency.
We recommended the management to stop from purchasing reconditioned motor vehicles moving forward. We also require the management to send written explanation on this procurement.
We recommended and the management agreed to revise the ordinance to fairly respond to the needs of constituents in crisis situations.

We recommended and the LDRRMO agreed to submit  the Monthly Reports on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF not later than the 15th day after the end of each month to enable the auditor to conduct the timely review, verification and monitoring of the sources and utilization of LDRRMF, thus deficiencies if any, will be informed to management for the latter’s immediate and appropriate action.

We recommended that management discontinue the practice of granting cash advances to accountable officers in amounts exceeding their corresponding maximum cash accountability. Otherwise, the management increase the fidelity bond of the AO to cater to the maximum cash accountability per cash advance drawn.

We recommended that management demand the return of mobilization fee from the contractor or payment from the insurance company whichever is faster and convenient. We also recommended to have the contractor blacklisted as a consequence for its default.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) of Taxpayer’s Index Card and furnish the Municipal Accountant copy of the list to serve as the basis in recording the RPT Receivable and SET Receivable accounts in the books pursuant to Section 19 and 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1, in order to reflect in the books accurate amounts for RPT and SET Receivables.

We required that the Municipal Accountant trace the transaction documents that pertain to financial assistance and return the balances to the SAs. For those projects where the General Fund was used to open the bank account, facilitate the return of the amount to the General Fund corresponding to the opening balance and return the interest income earned to the Bureau of Treasury. Henceforth, ensure the financial assistance are properly monitored and comply all conditions stipulated in the Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended management to formulate strategies and policies that will ensure an efficient and effective collection system, such as prompt assessment of new structures and frequent tax mapping to identify structures that need to be re-assessed or to be assessed so that the collection efficiency targets will be achieved.

We recommended that management restrain the placement of funds in high yield time deposits using the 20% LDF since these are not idle funds as defined in the aforementioned COA guidelines.  Further, ensure that with assistance of the MPDC and other key players in the decision making of the LGU to strategize so that projects identified have to be implemented within the year to address the needs of the community and to get the maximum value of fund allocated for a specific period considering the increasing prices of materials.  Further, conduct actual studies for previously identified projects and determine if those are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, consider for their reversion to release the funds and make available to some meaningful use.

We recommended that management provide justification on the above noted deficiency as well as the basis on how the above employees earns the 1.25 sick leave and 1.25 vacation leave credits if the employees were paid based on actual attendance. It is recommended that monthly rates be used in the payment of casual plantilla personnel. Also, review the respective job description of each employee and consider minimizing the actual numbers only to those which are urgent and necessary in the discharge of the operations of the respective offices so that benefits enjoyed are maximized. Finally, we recommend that management exert efforts to fill in the vacant regular positions which functions are already performed by the casual personnel.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and the LDRRMC adhere to the guidelines set under the pertinent sections of JMC No. 2013-1 in the appropriation and utilization of the LDRRMF. Further, funds from the General Fund have to be appropriated for other operational needs of the MDRRM Office.

We recommended that management closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned program/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre disaster preparedness programs so that intended purpose is attained/achieved as required Section 4.0 of NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG JMC No. 2013-1.

We recommended that management direct the LDRRMO to religiously follow the PPAs incorporated in the approved LDRRMFIP. Henceforth, in case there is a need to charge a PPA other than those included in the LDRRMFIP against the LDRRM funds, the LDRRMFIP has to be revised first and approved by the Sangguniang Bayan to accommodate such PPA.

We recommended that the management ensure that the computation of the amount of travel claims by the personnel is in accordance with the provisions set forth in Executive Order No. 77. Henceforth, for travels within the 50-kilometer radius, incidental expenses has to be excluded and the amount for the meal do not exceed thirty percent (30%) of the meal component of the DTE.

We recommended that management require the personnel concerned to refund the overpayment of AHDP. Henceforth, we recommend the Municipal Mayor to ensure that the LGU strictly complies to pertinent laws, rules and regulations when granting benefits to personnel in order to maintain efficiency in the utilization of government funds and to avoid irregular expenditures and disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer facilitates the remittance/release of the barangay shares from collection of Basic RPT, including interest thereon, directly to the barangay treasurer on a quarterly basis within five days after the end of each quarter without any lien or holdback for whatever purpose pursuant to Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to remit the Barangay shares on sand and gravel and real property taxes within the reglementary period pursuant to Section 271 of RA 7160. We also recommend for proper coordination between the Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to facilitate releases to avoid possible accumulation of unremitted collections.

It is recommended that all municipal officials and employees of LGU, Catigbian, and Bohol refund the amount of ₱712, 400.00 for the amount they received as CNA Incentive. Henceforth, abide by the rules and regulations in the granting of the CNA Incentive.

We recommended that Management demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support the release of financial aid to the latter and uphold observance of accountability and transparency of all government transactions.

We recommended that management:

Terminate and settle these trust funds in accordance with the   guidelines or agreement for which they were set-up or received.  Unutilized balances of the Trust Funds shall be returned to the trustor in order to relieve the municipality of the responsibility.

In cases that these accounts are balances of completed/ accomplished projects/programs of prior years, require the municipal accountant to revert to Retained Operating Surplus by certifying that these accounts have been outstanding for over two (2) years period

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of the supplies inventory and property, plant and equipment of the LGU, prepare the inventory reports and submit a copy thereof to COA in order to verify compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the RPCPPE with accounting records and effect adjustments on any noted discrepancies between the actual and recorded balances of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Management has put into plan the conduct of the inventory but due to Typhoon Odette, it was set aside as the inventory and assessment of damaged properties and households was urgent and necessary. Still, we will adhere to the recommendation.
observations.reason: No PPE reports were submitted as of CY2021.

Require the property officer to trace the property cards of the unserviceable properties and determine the relevant characteristics of the item to facilitate in the identification of its acquisition costs. Once the values of the properties are now identified/determined, pursue the required procedures in disposal of assets in accordance with the aforecited COA Circular 89-296. For unestablished acquisition costs, the Appraisal Committee shall determine the cost/value of the unserviceable properties. 2. Direct the accountable officials in possession of unserviceable property to submit to the Disposal Committee the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP). 3. Delete from the RPCPPE the IIRUP for condemned properties which are already approved by the Head of Office upon the recommendation of the Property Inspector and/or the Appraisal Committee and cancel the corresponding AREs. observations.managementAction: The LGU has already identified the assets for disposal and has prepared the first batch of assets to be disposed. However, when the pandemic hit and other emergencies occurred, the main focus was diverted to disaster response to address the immediate needs of the constituents. Nonetheless, we will continue the disposal this year.
observations.reason: Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) for CY 2021 was not submitted to the office.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in order to support the release of financial aid to the latter and uphold observance of accountability and transparency of all government transactions.
observations.managementAction: The different leagues are already registered with the Bureau of Internal Revenue thus their official receipts are with authority to print from BIR. We cannot require these leagues to issue Official Receipt of the Government since the leagues are not government agencies and the officials of the League are not bonded with the Bureau of Treasury.
observations.reason: Local officials have no control on the kind of official receipts issued to them during conventions and seminars.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend  that  management  require  the  Inventory  Committee  to
prioritize and complete the physical inventory within  a  reasonable  period
in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006. Also, management  is  enjoined
to ensure the full implementation of “One-time cleansing  of  PPE  accounts”
as provided for in the same circular.

We recommend that management require the personnel concerned to refund
 the overpayment of AHDP. Henceforth, we recommend the  Municipal  Mayor  to
 ensure that  the  LGU  strictly  complies  to  pertinent  laws,  rules  and
 regulations when granting  benefits  to  personnel  in  order  to  maintain
 efficiency in the utilization of government funds and  to  avoid  irregular
 expenditures and disallowances in audit.

We recommend  that  management  require  the  municipal  treasurer  to
monitor all financial transactions and see to it that balances of  all  bank
accounts  are  within  the  maintaining  balance  to  avoid  surcharges  and
penalties.  Further, we  require  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to  refund  the
amount of Ᵽ4,000.00. Likewise, require  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to  adopt
internal  control  measures  to  preclude  the  incurrence  of   unnecessary
expenditures and install proper controls and procedures in  the  payment  of
obligations and in the monitoring of the respective cash in bank balances.

We recommend management to require the Municipal accountant to see  to
it that  warranty  securities  posted  are  in  accordance  with  the  rates
prescribed in Section  62.2.3.3.1  of  RA  9184.   Henceforth,  require  the
contractors to post additional warranty security to cover  the  deficiencies
posted for projects that are still within the warranty period.

We recommend that the municipal officials review and assess  the  CEAP
Trust Fund specifically  the  policies,  conditions,  and  criteria  in  the
availment  of  the  scholarship  program  and  consider  adjustment  of  the
appropriation to address  other  priority  projects  that  can  benefit  the
public. Further, we require the Scholarship Board in coordination  with  the
Municipal Treasurer to submit the utilization report of the trust fund.

We recommend that the Municipal  Accountant  and  Municipal  Treasurer
facilitates the remittance/release of the barangay  shares  from  collection
of  Basic  RPT,  including  interest  thereon,  directly  to  the   barangay
treasurer on a quarterly basis within five days after the end  of  each
quarter without any lien  or  holdback  for  whatever  purpose  pursuant  to
Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer  to
remit the Barangay shares on sand and gravel and real property taxes  within
the reglementary period  pursuant  to  Section  271  of  RA  7160.  We  also
recommend for  proper  coordination  between  the  Municipal  Treasurer  and
Accountant  to  facilitate  releases  to  avoid  possible  accumulation   of
unremitted collections.

We recommend that the Municipal officials exercise fiscal restraint in
the disbursement of  funds  and  fast  track  the  implementation  of  these
priority projects  so  that  the  intended  beneficiaries  could  enjoy  the
benefits derived therefrom. Likewise, submit a written  explanation  on  the
above discrepancy.

We recommend that the management   trace the period or date of   acquisition of the assets and   compute the depreciation based on the estimated useful life of assets as provided under COA Circular 2017-004. Prior year’s depreciation have to be taken up as Prior Years   Adjustment to be closed to   Government Equity and current   year’s depreciation are to be   recorded as expense. Accordingly, the following journal entries shall be made
observations.managementAction: Necessary entries were made to reflect depreciation expense in the books.
observations.reason: Depreciation expense in the GF and SEF books was recognized for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that all collections be remitted intact and daily to avoid misuse of government funds.  Likewise, management should call the attention of the above collectors to remind their duties and responsibilities to ensure safety and proper accountability of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Collectors were reminded to remit their collections daily.
observations.reason: All collections were remitted intact and were turned over on time.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management abide by the prescribed rates of Magna Carta Benefit for Public Health Workers as stipulated in DBM-DOH Joint Circular No. 1 of 2012. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to check and refer from time to time different government rules and issuances that are applicable to disbursements to ensure that funds are used in accordance with accounting and auditing rules.
observations.managementAction: Subsequent payments starting September 2020 were in accordance with the new guidelines.
observations.reason: Recommendations were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the BAC submit explanation why the contract was not awarded to the lowest bidder.  Further, review properly the price quotations among the suppliers to obtain the price most advantageous to the government.
observations.managementAction: The management refunded the price difference between the lowest supplier and the awarded supplier as recommended by the audit team.
observations.reason: An official receipt was provided with regards to the refund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management direct those responsible for the procurement to submit written justification to the noted price variance and defying the DOH Memorandum Nos. 2020-0058-B and 2020-0014A that were issued at the height of the pandemic. Henceforth, require the end –users and the BAC to abide and be updated with rules and regulations related to COVID-19 procurement for the prevention of abuses and corruption on the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: For CY 2021, purchase of medical supplies were made in accordance with the price range in DOH memo with an allowance for the transportation cost.
observations.reason: It is reminded that the management, in all instances, shall ensure to avail the most advantageous prevailing price in the market.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the agency observe the guidelines stipulated under DBM LBC No.125 as to the utilization of BGCM Funds. Henceforth, we recommend that Management ensure that government funds are protected and properly utilized giving priority to more important projects, programs and activities. Further, we recommend that the cost for the refrigerator and TV are to be appropriately charged to the GF Proper.
observations.managementAction: The cost of refrigerator and TV were appropriately charged to the GF Proper as recommended.
observations.reason: Recommendations were implemented.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the legal basis of the payment of cash incentives to above mentioned personnel. Management is likewise advised to stop the practice of granting incentives in the absence of statutory basis. Furthermore, management is reminded to observe with the provisions of COA Circular 2012-003 in spending government funds.
observations.managementAction: The management followed the advice of the auditors and end the payment of such incentives.
observations.reason: For CY 2021, payment of such cash incentives is no longer granted.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that HRMO and the municipal accountant require all contractual employees to attach accomplishment reports for payment of their monthly honoraria and that entries in the biometric data has to be the exact information to be transferred to the DTR to avoid inconsistencies and doubtful rendition of service.
observations.managementAction: The auditor’s recommendation is followed.
observations.reason: DTRs submitted were reviewed and found in order.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant investigate the negative balances of RPT receivables/Deferred RPT and effect the necessary adjusting entries in the agency books to be able to present the accounts fairly in the financial statements. In addition, direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer the booking up of RPT Receivables at the beginning of the year based on Certified List of Taxpayers to ensure attainable collection targets and compliance with the applicable guidelines and avoid negative balances in the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant traced and located the cause of unreconciled balances of RPT and SET receivables.

observations.recommendation: We require the Municipal Accountant to locate and trace the causes of said unreconciled balances of RPT and SET receivables and effect the adjustments/corrections of the affected accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Office of the Municipal Accountant traced and located the cause of unreconciled balances of RPT and SET receivables.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2021, the Real Property Tax (RPT) Receivable and Special Education Tax (SET) Receivable balances are now reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Accounting Department properly check the Fund charges made by the Municipal Budget Office to avoid errors in both the Treasury and Accounting records. Henceforth, the Disbursing Officer should observe due diligence in the recording of transactions to avoid errors and facilitate accurate recording of balances in the cashbook. Further, the Accounting Department and Disbursing Officer should conduct reconciliation of balances at month-end to determine any differences and effect appropriate adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The recommendation of the auditor is well taken. Proper Fund in charging of accounts is carefully determined with the coordination of the Municipal Budget Office and Accounting Office.
observations.reason: During 2021 Cash Examination, monthly balances between the cashbook and general ledger balances are reconciled.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to locate and trace the causes of said unadjusted balances of the liability account.  Henceforth, it is reminded that income has to be recognized in year when earned in order to reflect the correct and accurate balances thereof.
observations.managementAction: Adjusting entries recommended by the auditor were effected. Other Deferred Credit already earned were recognized and adjusted to Prior Year Adjustments. New collections regarding to advance payments of taxpayers accumulated to the current balance of the account.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2021, Other Deferred Credit balance is P477,704.75.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended to management with the assistance of the MPDC that projects identified have to be implemented within the year to deliver the intended benefits to the people and to get the maximum value of fund utilization.  It is recommended to conduct actual studies for previously identified projects and determine if those are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, realign funds to other projects attune with the present needs.
observations.managementAction: The development projects under 20% for CY 2018 and prior were already implemented. The balances represent savings from the implementation and these are to be realigned for other projects deemed beneficial to the constituents.
observations.reason: As of December 2020, From the ₱26,853,852.79 unutilized 20% DF, ₱23,782,003.72 were implemented. Only ₱768,858.13 remained unutilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM planned program/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre disaster preparedness programs so that intended purpose is attained/achieved as required in Section 1 and 2 of Rule 18, Republic Act 10121.
observations.managementAction: LDRRM Funds was maximized.
observations.reason: LDRRM funds budgeted for 2021 was fully utilized.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provide justification on the above noted deficiency. Further, it is recommended that monthly rates be used in the payment of casual plantilla personnel. Provide also basis on how the above employees earns the 1.25 sick leave and 1.25 vacation leave credits if the employees were paid based on actual attendance.  Finally, review the respective job description of each employee and consider minimizing the actual numbers only to those which are urgent and necessary in the discharge of the operations of the respective offices, so that benefits enjoyed are maximized.
observations.managementAction: Due to PS limitation, the casual plantilla personnel are given leave credits, year-end bonus, cash gift and service recognition incentive (SRI).
observations.reason: We reiterate our recommendation to review the respective job description of each employee and consider minimizing the actual numbers only to those which are urgent and necessary in the discharge of the operations of the respective offices, so that benefits enjoyed are maximized.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management assist Councilor Lastimosa and those others concerned with similar cases in the LGU, to make representations with the GSIS for the reimbursement of the premiums paid.  Further, we require the AAO to update membership status for all GSIS members particularly those newly hired, re-entered, transferred or retired to avoid any discrepancy and inconvenience to the members.  Finally, require the AAO to coordinate with GSIS for the reimbursement of the government share that was erroneously remitted to GSIS similar to the case of Councilor Lastimosa and Mr. Bolante and that of the government shares be returned to the LGU.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to comply with the auditor’s recommendation.
observations.reason: Management assured to assist SB Lastimosa so that he can be refunded of his GSIS remittance and coordinate with the GSIS on how can the LGU claim the government share.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit a list of recipients per barangay indicating therein the specific relief goods distributed and received with indication on dates of receipt, among other information to make possible the proper accounting and recording of procured relief goods. Furthermore, the management has to establish control procedures to regulate the distributions of relief goods to actual beneficiaries and maintains a proper record keeping as to the acquisition, issuance, as well as the balances of inventory of the relief goods to avoid wastage or loss of government funds.
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to comply with the auditor’s recommendation.
observations.reason: Management has initially submitted distribution list per barangay.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management require personnel concerned to support with proper documents OT services rendered in accordance with CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2015 on the policies and guidelines on overtime services of government personnel and to adhere to Section 4(6) of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management complied and submitted the required documents.
observations.reason: The documentary requirements were submitted and complied. However, the management failed to comply the Reportorial Requirements of Overtime Services as stated in Sec 12.0 of CSC-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2015.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local chief executive require the persons liable to immediately settle the disallowances, otherwise initiate administrative and/or criminal action against them in line with Section 7 of the 2009 RRSA.

observations.reason: NSSDCs were issued for partial settlement of NDs. Accounting records and Auditor’s file are subject for reconciliation as to the balances per ND.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management remind all officials and employees to submit liquidation reports within 30 days for travel and other time-bound undertakings. Also, ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of each year. Otherwise, direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately withhold the payment of their salaries or any amount due them. Further, require the Municipal Accountant to demand liquidation and/or refund from the former employees of the LGU for their unliquidated cash advances.

observations.reason: Aurilio Dinorog, Frank Ordiz, and Ador Suaybaguio remained unliquidated at audit year-end.

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that all municipal officials and employees of LGU, Catigbian, and Bohol refund the amount of P712, 400.00 for the amount they received as CNA Incentive. Henceforth, abide by the rules and regulations in the granting of the CNA Incentive.
observations.managementAction: The municipal officials and employees had already refunded the amount. We will submit the necessary documents to comply with the settlement of the disallowance.
observations.reason: NSSDCs were issued for partial settlement of the ND. Accounting records and Auditor’s file are subject for reconciliation with the balance of P47,800.00 with the latest issued NSSDC 22-001 partially lifting it amounting to P39,307.50.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management instruct the municipal accountant to review and check the status of the listing/schedule of accounts payable to determine if there are valid claims against these obligated amounts, and if none, these should be reverted immediately to the general fund for subsequent budgeting.
observations.managementAction: Management still needs more time to trace the subsidiary ledgers of these accounts because these accounts existed for a long time.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: Create a task force to identify the lots owned by the LGU and review if the same are titled in the name of the LGU; Require the task force to determine the beneficial owners or actual users of these lots and to negotiate for just compensation if feasible, or work on the transfer of those actually occupied by other government agencies in the LGU through the passage of an ordinance by the Sangguniang Bayan donating them to the agencies concerned; Require the task force to work on the titling of all LGU-owned lots. observations.managementAction: Some of these lots were already surveyed by the surveyor and are now in the course of titling. We implement it in batches because of the cost to be incurred in titling these lots.
observations.reason: Other LGU officials are not agreeable to the idea of donating LGU-owned lots to the other agencies concerned.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant investigate the negative
balances of RPT receivables/Deferred RPT and effect the necessary adjusting
entries in the agency books to be able to present the accounts fairly in the financial
statements. In addition, direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the
Municipal Treasurer the booking up of RPT Receivables at the beginning of the
year based on Certified List of Taxpayers to ensure attainable collection targets
and compliance with the applicable guidelines and avoid negative balances in the
affected accounts.

We require the Municipal Accountant to locate and trace the causes of said
unreconciled balances of RPT and SET receivables and effect the
adjustments/corrections of the affected accounts.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to
locate and trace the causes of said unadjusted balances of the aforementioned
liability account. Henceforth, it is reminded that income has to be recognized in
year when earned in order to reflect the correct and accurate balances thereof.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to
observe appropriate use of accounts to present fairly the financial statements of the
LGU. Registration fees from trainings/conventions and seminars/workshops should
be recorded as Training expenses, rather than Traveling Expenses pursuant to the
account description provided in COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory
Committee to conduct a complete physical count of the supplies inventory and
property, plant and equipment of the LGU, prepare the inventory reports and
submit a copy thereof to COA in order to verify compliance with Section 124 of the
NGAS Manual. Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant reconcile
the RPCPPE with accounting records and effect adjustments on any noted
discrepancies between the actual and recorded balances of the affected accounts.

We recommend the Management:
1. Require the property officer to trace the property cards of the unserviceable
properties and determine the relevant characteristics of the item to facilitate
in the identification of its acquisition costs. Once the values of the properties
are now identified/determined, pursue the required procedures in disposal
of assets in accordance with the aforecited COA Circular 89-296. For
unestablished acquisition costs, the Appraisal Committee shall determine
the cost/value of the unserviceable properties.
2. Direct the accountable officials in possession of unserviceable property to
submit to the Disposal Committee the Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property (IIRUP).
3. Delete from the RPCPPE the IIRUP for condemned properties which are
already approved by the Head of Office upon the recommendation of the
Property Inspector and/or the Appraisal Committee and cancel the
corresponding AREs. Copy of approved IIRUP shall be furnished to the
COA. However, the Accounting Office shall not drop the approved IIRUP
from the accounting records until such time the Auction Sale is complete.

We recommend that Management require the Property Officer to prepare and
issue Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) to officials and
employees who are in actual custody of the equipment. Also require the renewal of
the ARE every three (3) years pursuant to existing regulation to safeguard
government funds and property.

We recommend that issuances and inventory of all accountable forms and cash
tickets be regularly checked and monitored to ensure these are all properly and
accurately accounted for.

We recommend that the Accounting Department properly check the Fund
charges made by the Municipal Budget Office to avoid errors in both the Treasury
and Accounting records. Henceforth, the Disbursing Officer should observe due
diligence in the recording of transactions to avoid errors and facilitate accurate
recording of balances in the cashbook. Further, the Accounting Department and
Disbursing Officer should conduct reconciliation of balances at month-end to
determine any differences and effect appropriate adjustments.

We recommend that management prioritize and consider filling –up the
vacant positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs,
plans and activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the
constituents.

Further, personnel movement has to be effected in order to acknowledge the
exemplary performance of employees by way of promotion to higher rank thereby
allowing the entry of new applicants for lower level positions.

We recommend that management make sure that fuel consumption shall be
properly controlled and accounted for through approved Requisition and Issue
Voucher. In addition, comply with pertinent section of COA Circular No.77-61 in
accomplishing the Driver’s Trip Ticket. Please exercise an efficient control in the
use of the gasoline to avoid wastage of government funds.

We recommend that HRMO and the municipal accountant require all
contractual employees to attach accomplishment reports for payment of their
monthly honoraria and that entries in the biometric data has to be the exact
information to be transferred to the DTR to avoid inconsistencies and doubtful
rendition of service.

We recommend that management require the
Municipal Accountant to use the proper
accounts in recording financial transactions
to avoid misstatement of the financial
statements and effect the necessary
adjustments. Further, require that the
interest income recorded in the Trust Fund
be transferred and recognized in the General
Fund as surplus, so that this can be
appropriated for programs, projects and
activities beneficial to the public.
observations.managementAction: 2018 adjustments were recorded in the effected. Trust Fund were transferred to the General Fund at year end.
observations.reason: Interest income adjustments were recorded in the Trust Fund were transferred to the General Fund at year end.

observations.recommendation:
We require that management remind the
municipal accountant to prepare and submit
to COA all bank reconciliation statements
within the prescribed period to ensure that
cash balances reflected on the financial
statements are reliable and accurate.

We recommend that Management instruct
the Municipal Accountant to submit monthly
trial balances and journals on the 20th day of
the following month as duly required in the
NGAS Manual to facilitate the verification
of transactions and reports of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Reports were delivered mostly 3 months later than the statement date.
observations.reason: Nonetheless, the recommendation of the auditor is noted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management require the
municipal treasurer to secure Official
Receipts for all funds released to other
government agency and the accountant has
to record such as receivables and not as
rather direct debit to expenses account, and
require the liquidation thereof.
observations.managementAction: The management and auditor agreed that the financial assistance will be paid to the scholar that directly paid to the school. However such assistance is usually processed later in the semester and the scholar had already paid his/her obligations to the school so the payee is now changed to the name of the scholar. So we cannot, at all times, require the Official Receipt from the school.
observations.reason: It is stated in the 2018 Memorandum of Agreement that the financial assistance will be paid to the school where she enrolled. However such assistance is usually processed later in the semester and the scholar had already paid his/her obligations to the school so the payee is now changed to the name of the scholar. So we cannot, at all times, require the Official Receipt from the school.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Bids and Awards
Committee adopt the earliest timeframe for
the completion of the bidding process to
ensure faster delivery of services intended
for the constituents.

observations.reason: Review of the timeliness of the procurement bidding process for 2019 were in accordance with the timeframe required in the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

municipal accountant to deduct the

future billing of the contractor.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management implement
immediately and accordingly the purpose of
trust receipts. Otherwise, evaluate the need
of these unexpended balances of
aid/financial assistance from different source
agencies. Should these funds prove to be no

surplus of the General Fund.

We recommend that the Municipal
Accountant take up the transfer of funds as

observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant adjusted the recording of the asset to Construction in Progress and completed projects were turned over to the barangays with complete documentation for recording.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management has to
ensure that all its employees are able to
receive a minimum of ₱5,000 take home pay
per month for them to maintain self-respect
and dignity as employees of the government,
in pursuance of Section 48 of the 2018 GAA.
Likewise, we recommend that the
Municipal Accountant and HRMO monitor
payroll deductions and refuse to sign
certifications for application of loans should
an employee net pay fall below ₱5,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Employees were informed of the required minimum net take home pay of P5,000.00 per month and observed in their payroll during post-audit of transactions.
observations.reason: Minimum of P5,000.00 net take home pay of the employee per month were observed in their payroll during post-audit of transactions.

credits be allowed once a year only as

Concern of the employees were noted. Thus, we leave the discretion to the management.
observations.reason: Auditors reiterated that employees are only allowed to monetize a maximum of thirty (30) days as long as there shall be at least five (5) vacation leave/service credit after monetization.

Omnibus Rules on Leave.

observations.reason: Earned leave credits by the employees paid based on actual attendance will be based on the tables provided in the Omnibus Rules on Leave as per advice of the auditor.

actual studies for previously identified
projects and determine if those are still
feasible and practicable to be implemented,

observations.recommendation:

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that Management remind all officials and employees to submit
liquidation reports within 30 days for travel and other time-bound undertakings. Also,
ensure that all cash advances are fully liquidated at the end of each year. Otherwise,
direct the Municipal Accountant to immediately withhold the payment of their salaries
or any amount due them.

Further, require the Municipal Accountant to demand liquidation and/or refund
from the former employees of the LGU for their unliquidated cash advances.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Accountant to use the
proper accounts in recording financial transactions to avoid misstatement of the
financial statements and effect the necessary adjustments. Further, require that the
interest income recorded in the Trust Fund be transferred and recognized in the
General Fund as surplus, so that this can be appropriated for programs, projects and
activities beneficial to the public.

We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee
to conduct a complete physical count of the supplies inventory and property, plant and
equipment of the LGU, prepare the inventory reports and submit a copy thereof to
COA in order to verify compliance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual. Likewise, we
recommend that the Municipal Accountant perform reconciliation of the RPCPPE with
accounting records and effect adjustments on any noted discrepancies between the
actual and recorded balances of the affected accounts.

We recommend that management:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant to locate and trace the causes of said
unreconciled balances of the reciprocal accounts and effect the
adjustments/corrections of the affected accounts.
2. For transactions which are not proper charges to a certain fund, require the
Municipal Treasurer to prepare a check to immediately refund erroneous
charging.
3. Require the Municipal Treasurer to be cautious in issuing check payments for all
transactions to avoid erroneous charging against each fund maintained.

We recommend that management implement immediately and accordingly the
purpose of trust receipts. Otherwise, evaluate the need of these unexpended balances of
aid/financial assistance from different source agencies. Should these funds prove to be
no longer needed, revert to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant take up the transfer of funds as
receivable from the barangays to ensure proper monitoring of the project and validate
if the purpose has been met. Finally, when adjustments are being effected, determine
the ownership of the completed projects. If owned by the barangay, the projects have to be
turned over, with complete documentation of the assets, and properly recorded in the books
of the respective barangays.

We recommend that management provide justification on the above noted
deficiency. Further, it is recommended that monthly rates be used in the payment of
casual plantilla personnel. Provide also basis on how the above employees earns the 1.25
sick leave and 1.25 vacation leave credits if the employees were paid based on actual
attendance. Finally, review the respective job description of each employee and
consider minimizing the actual numbers only to those which are urgent and necessary
in the discharge of the operations of the respective offices, so that benefits enjoyed are
maximized.

We recommend that management has to ensure that all its employees are able to
receive a minimum of ₱5,000 take home pay per month for them to maintain self-
respect and dignity as employees of the government, in pursuance of Section 48 of the
2018 GAA.

Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Accountant and HRMO monitor
payroll deductions and refuse to sign certifications for application of loans should an
employee net pay fall below ₱5,000.00.

We recommend that management provide basis on how the above employees
earns the 1.25 sick leave and 1.25 vacation leave credits if the employees were paid
based on actual attendance. If no budgetary constraints, casual plantilla personnel
maybe paid on a monthly wage instead of daily wages, otherwise, earned leave credits
will be based on the tables provided in the Omnibus Rules on Leave.

We recommend that management ensure that complete plans and the detailed
engineering designs be approved before the conduct of the bidding of any projects.
Further, we request that the lacking documents be submitted to facilitate the review of
the price reasonableness of the contract.

We recommend that issuances and inventory of all accountable forms and cash
tickets be regularly checked and monitored to ensure these are all properly and accurately
accounted for. Further, segregation of the collecting and disbursing functions has to be
effected immediately and designate other personnel to handle the cash tickets to avoid any
future cash ticket sales shortages.

We require that management remind the municipal accountant to prepare and
submit to COA all bank reconciliation statements within the prescribed period to ensure
that cash balances reflected on the financial statements are reliable and accurate.

We recommend that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to submit
monthly trial balances and journals on the 20th day of the following month as duly
required in the NGAS Manual to facilitate the verification of transactions and reports
of the LGU.

We recommend that management require the municipal treasurer to secure
Official Receipts for all funds released to other government agency and the accountant
has to record such as receivables and not as direct debit to expenses account, and
require the liquidation thereof.

We recommend that the local chief executive require the persons liable to
immediately settle the disallowances, otherwise initiate administrative and/or criminal
action against them in line with Section 7 of the 2009 RRSA.

We recommend that monetization of leave credits be allowed once a year only as
required by laws, rules and regulations.

We recommend that management closely monitor and ensure that the LDRRM
planned program/projects/activities are implemented and give due regard on the
maximum utilization of the 70% for pre disaster preparedness programs so that
intended purpose is attained/achieved as required Section 1 and 2 of Rule 18, Republic
Act 10121.

We recommend the Municipal Accountant to require Official Receipts (OR) for
all claims of taxi fares even if the amount is only ₱75.00 to avoid disallowance in audit.

accumulated depreciation accounts in

assets in the financial statements of the
municipality

Be stringent in giving authority to his officials and employees, and it should be anchored on valid, legal, and proper procedures and norms. b.)
