Finally, submit legal basis for the DILG Officer to join the post-qualification of the contractor which is not part of the mandate of DILG and also present authority to travel from DILG-Region VII.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 the three (3) days leave of absence as sick leave were filed by the employees.
observations.reason: the three (3) days sick leave were filed but not deducted from the leave cards of concerned employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management demand for the issuance of an official receipt of the government by the recipient Leagues concerned in the order to support the release of financial aid to the latter and uphold observance of accountability and transparency of all government transactions.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Financial assistance still extended to the leagues because this is kind of commitment of each official.
observations.reason: Local officials have no control on the receipts issued to them during conventions and seminars.

observations.recommendation: a. Create an Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory especially after the 7.2 magnitude earthquake hit the municipality and damaged some of its buildings and structures; b. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct the physical count of all the properties recorded in the books and those not yet recorded but actually found in the LGU offices/premises and to prepare the corresponding inventory reports thereof; and reconcile them with the accounting records to come up with the correct account balances in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 There is already a list of inventory of properties but still has to reconcile with the accounting records.
observations.reason: Personnel were busy with the transition period from the earthquake that hit the Province of Bohol last October 2013. Office equipment and properties were still being segregated because some were already unserviceable due to the earthquake.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to trace the transactions recorded in both accounts and make the necessary corrections/adjustments. These accounts should be settled to allow the creditor-fund to utilize the amounts for their intended purpose, if any.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 The accountant has not completed tracing of the accounts.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous transactions.

observations.recommendation: a) Create a task force to identify the lots owned by the LGU and review if the same are titled in the name of the LGU; b) Require the task force to determine the beneficial owners or actual users of these lots and to negotiate for just compensation if feasible, or work on the transfer of those actually occupied by other government agencies in the LGU through the passage of an ordinance by the Sangguniang Bayan donating them to the agencies concerned; c) Require the task force to work on the titling of all LGU-owned lots. d) Require the Municipal Accountant to update/adjust the Land (201) account balance in the books of accounts based on the results of the review of JEV No. 2012-12-04033 and the identification of municipal-owned lots and its titling.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 The Land account balance had already been adjusted by the Municipal Accountant but only to the error in taking up the amount of increase. The former mayor assigned the municipal assessor to undertake this task.
observations.reason: Other LGU officials are not agreeable to the idea of donating them to the other agencies concerned.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant attach necessary documents/schedules/computations to support all the transactions recorded in the General Journal taking up the Depreciation Expenses.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management: a.) Terminate and settle these trust funds in accordance with the guidelines or agreement for which they were set-up or received. Unutilized balances of the Trust Funds shall be returned to the trustor in order to relieve the municipality of the responsibility. b.) In cases, that these accounts are balances of completed/accomplished projects/programs of prior years, require the municipal accountant to revert to Retained Operating Surplus by certifying that these accounts have been outstanding for over two (2) years period.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2011

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommend that management require the accountant to review the balances
of the Due to NGAS account and effect correct adjustments and reclassifications, if
warranted.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management:
1. Require the Municipal Accountant to locate and trace the causes of said
unreconciled balances of the intra-agency receivable/payable account and effect the
reconciliation of the affected accounts.
2. For transactions paid from certain funds which are not proper charges to the fund,
require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a check to immediately refund
erroneous charging.
3. Require the Municipal Treasurer to be cautious in issuing check payments for all
transactions to avoid erroneous charging.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the LGU stop payment of government share for the
contributions of all PAG-IBIG members in excess of P100.00 per employee. Further,
effect the refund of government share of the CY 2016 and previous years’ excessive
contributions of employers share to PAG-IBIG.

Recommendation:
It is recommended to management with the assistance of the MPDC that
projects identified have to be implemented within the year to deliver the intended
benefits to the people and to get the maximum value of fund utilization considering the
increasing prices of materials in order to address the needs of the community.
Further, conduct actual studies for previously identified projects and determine if
those are still feasible and practicable to be implemented, otherwise, realign funds to
other projects attune with the present needs.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator
explain why the projects were not listed and specified in the Annual Budget.
Henceforth, formulation of future annual budgets has to include identification and
specification of projects particularly for the barangays.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Local School Board see to it that funds allocated for
Special Education Fund should be budgeted and utilized in accordance with Section
272 of RA 7160 and the rules and regulations issued under the DECS (now Department
of Education), DBM and DILG Joint Circular Nos. 01, 01-A and 01-B to effectively
attain the purpose for which the fund was created and avoid disallowance in audit.
Security Services and support staff’s salary may be appropriated out of the General
Fund.

Recommendation:
We recommend that the Human Resource Management Officer conform to the
policies and standards of the Civil Service Commission’s Omnibus Rules on Leave
regarding the leave credits of its officials and employees to avoid disallowance in audit.
Further, require the concerned officials to immediately refund of the money value of
the unrecorded sick leave credits in relation to the observation.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management prioritize and consider filling –up the vacant
positions to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and
activities of the local government unit for the optimal benefit of the constituents.
Further, personnel movement has to be effected in order to acknowledge the
exemplary performance of employees by way of promotion to higher rank thereby
allowing the entry of new applicants for lower level positions.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management prioritize and fast track the regularization
and fill up the LDRRMO II to head the LDRRMO in compliance with NDRRMC,
DILG, DBM, CSC JMC 2014-1.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management mobilize personnel to study and monitor
whether projects are still feasible to be implemented, otherwise, select other projects
that are viable and beneficial for the community.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management monitor l the use of the telephones by setting
up control measures to prevent unauthorized use. The billing statements for all calls
need to be identified based on the logbook entries whether official or personal and only
official calls must be paid by the government.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management control/limit expenses on training materials to
ensure that government funds are not wasted.

Recommendation:
We recommend that management:
a. Require all drivers/ vehicle users concerned to properly and completely fill-up the
trip tickets for every trips undertaken and attach all trip tickets to the disbursement
vouchers and adhere strictly to the guidelines promulgated in the use of government
vehicles by stating all required data and require all passengers to sign the trip
tickets and submit to the Commission On Audit the Monthly Report of Fuel
Consumption and Monthly Report of Official Travels within ten (10) days of the
succeeding month for validation.
b. Explain on the doubtful validity of fuel in tank balances of the Mitsubishi Delica
van.

Recommendation:
It is recommended for management to continue to implement and sustain the
effective implementation of the solid waste management plan to promote a clean,
healthy and safe environment and avoid any untoward consequences brought about by
improper waste disposal. Further, management is required to explain why there were
several pieces of unused RCB in the dump-site.

We require that management observe the guidelines provided in DOF Circular 001-2015. If there are issues that need to be resolved and management still opts to maintain its deposits with the private bank, they have to comply with a the conditions in Section 5.4 to 5.5 in the aforementioned circular, otherwise the DOF may impose the sanctions stipulated in Section 6 hereof.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Trust Fund account at FCB Catigbian was closed and transferred to Postal Bank last August 17, 2016. However, only the Gen. Fund Proper SEF FCB bank accounts were retained for security reasons. However, bank balances were monitored that it will be below ₱500,000.00

observations.recommendation: It is recommended that all municipal officials and employees of LGU, Catigbian, Bohol refund the amount of P712,400.00 they received as CNA Incentive. Henceforth, abide by the rules and regulations in the granting of the CNA Incentive.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 The management is deducting the CNA disallowance in a staggered basis.
observations.reason: Personnel cannot refund in one time because of financial constraints.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to reassess all business establishments concerned and collect deficiency taxes from them including previous years’ under-collection. Henceforth, ensure that establishments be correctly assessed to be able to impose the correct taxes from them. Finally, the 2006 Revenue Code has to be revised to achieve optimum collection efficiency for the municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2015 Revised Revenue Code implemented on January 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management implement the intended projects for which the funds deposited were appropriated or created. Meanwhile, in instances when funds are determined to be idle or excess funds, it should be invested in government finance institutions and not in private banks.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Trust Fund account at FCB Catigbian was closed and transferred to Postal Bank last August 17, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the municipal accountant use the proper account in recording financial transactions to avoid misstatement of the financial statements. Meanwhile, the erroneous entry should be reclassified to its correct accounting entries in the ensuing year.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Interest income properly recorded.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management provide justification for the charging of the salaries of the casual employees against MOOE funds instead of against PS funds.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Some employees were already paid using the budget in the Personal Services.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that monetization of leave credits be allowed once a year only as required by laws, rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2014 Employees claimed monetization only once during the year.

observations.recommendation: Direct the Municipal Treasurer to stop immediately the practice of maintaining depository accounts with private banks and to secure approval from the Department of Finance if she has to maintain depository accounts other than the Land Bank, the Development Bank and other thrift banks, rural banks and cooperative banks authorized in the said order. Terminate time deposit placements at private banks. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to secure authority from the Sangguniang Bayan (SB) if she has to maintain time deposits with any bank.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Trust Fund account at FCB Catigbian was closed and transferred to Postal Bank last August 17, 2016.

observations.recommendation: a. Send demand letters to the accountable officers concerned and closely monitor the liquidation of these cash advances. b. Send tracer letters to the COA Central Office on its action on the liquidation documents of cash advances for intelligence/confidential funds of the LGU officials concerned; c. Exert more effort to collect these receivables such as withholding payments of claims of municipal officials/employees who have long overdue accountabilities with the LGU. d. Issue bills or collection/confirmation letters to agencies/persons concerned as proofs that the Municipality has exerted efforts/exhausted all means to collect these receivables that have become dormant. Make a letter-request to the Commission on Audit for their write-off pursuant to after exhausting all efforts to collect them.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Demand letters were sent to the accountable officers.
observations.reason: Most of the accountable officers were already retired from service and collection thereof is remote.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management instruct the municipal accountant to review and check the status of the listing/schedule of accounts payable to determine if there are valid claims against these obligated amounts, and if none, these should be reverted immediately to the general fund for subsequent budgeting.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2013 Dormant payables already existed in the books for quite some time and the accountant has no means to locate the vouchers since the assumed office only recently.

observations.recommendation: a. Stop immediately the practice of including the payments of loan amortizations to private lending institutions as deduction from the payroll. b. Terminate immediately the arrangement (MOA) with the FCB and with other private lending institutions. c. For regular employees who wish to avail of such loan facility, they may transact directly with the private lending institution concerned without involving the LGU personnel and resources to avoid incurring unnecessary expenses. d. Follow strictly the provisions of Section 262 of the GAAM, and the pertinent provisions of PD 1445 and RA 7160.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 Private loans will no longer be accommodated as payroll deductions in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: It is stated in the MOA between the LGU and lending institution that the amortizations will be through payroll deductions. Management was apprehensive of the consequences if a lawsuit will be filed against the municipality.

observations.recommendation: a.) Require the Municipal Accountant and all department heads to comply with the provisions of Section 114 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs specifically on the recording of supplies and materials as Inventories. All purchases of supplies and materials should be taken up as Inventories except those purchases from the Petty Cash Fund; b.) Require the Municipal Accountant to keep an updated Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC) and the General Services Officer to maintain Stock Cards for each inventory item received which should be reconciled with the SLC in the Accounting department; c.) Require the GSO to prepare and submit the Report on the Supplies and Materials Issued monthly to the Accounting department for the recording of the utilization in the Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the Inventories accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 The Accounting Unit started taking up purchases of office supplies under Inventories account.
observations.reason: Not yet started. Lack of personnel to maintain the subsidiary ledgers and stock cards.

observations.recommendation: a.) Require persons responsible for the transaction to submit an explanation/justification for entering into such repair contract; b.) Conduct proper reevaluation of the repair on the vehicle and if possible renegotiate with the contractor/repair shop owner the contract price based on the evaluation conducted; c.) Require the repair shop owner accreditation from the Department of Trade and Industry; d.) Henceforth, submit to COA all contracts, memorandum of agreements and the like, within five (5) days after perfection thereof, for review and evaluation pursuant to the aforementioned regulation, to protect the interest of the LGU; e.) Require the personnel concerned to prepare and submit to COA for technical review and inspection the documents enumerated in Annex Q.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2012 Copies of perfected contracts submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: In order to present a fair and reliable Financial Statement, we recommend that management: a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately adjust/reclassify accounts erroneously/ invalidly classified as Accounts Payable in conformity with the New Government Accounting System (NGAS). b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile with previous records/ vouchers to identify the specific creditors.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2010 The Municipal Accountant already adjusted some accounts erroneously classified as Accounts Payable such as unaccounted credit to cash in bank that income derived from the Dagook Adventure Park assistance received were still recorded under Accounts Payable amounting to P3,915.33; income from National Government Agencies and LGUs amounting to P1,645,478.05 subject to liquidation, totaling P862,958.41; donations intended for LAMP expenses amounting to P29,300.00 reclassified to Income account. The Municipal Accountant tried her best to locate the previous records for Accounts Payable unidentified creditors amounting to P461,903.18 carried over from previous years still exist in the books as of December 31, 2011 due to the failure to locate the records/ vouchers as basis for adjustment.
observations.reason: Accounts payable are current and less than two (2) years.
